item.title: Time trends in strategies for postoperative Crohn's disease management and long-term surgical recurrence: ENEIDA registry data.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The availability of biologicals has been associated with a decreased rate of first intestinal resection in patients with Crohn's disease (CD). However, its impact on postoperative recurrence (POR) ...

item.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease Is Associated With an Increased Risk of End-Stage Renal Disease: A Population-Based Cohort Study.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is associated with renal complications, but population-based data on end-stage renal disease (ESRD) are limited. To evaluate the incidence of ESRD and renal transpl...

item.title: Selective COX-2 inhibitors for short-term musculoskeletal pain in inflammatory bowel disease in remission: a narrative review.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - Review - Selective cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitors are often considered when anti-inflammatory analgesia is needed for musculoskeletal pain in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but concern...

item.title: Nutrition and Body Composition: Missing Outcomes in Appetite- and Weight-Modifying Therapies for Individuals with Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
A Longitudinal Disease Progression Model Characterizing Endoscopic Response and Remission in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Journal of pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics - RCT - Crohn's disease (CD) is a heterogeneous, progressive inflammatory bowel disease in which substantial placebo effects often confound assessment of therapeutic efficacy. A placebo-based disease model...

item.title: Acneiform Eruption with Deucravacitinib: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Clinical and experimental dermatology - Deucravacitinib, an oral selective tyrosine kinase 2 (TYK2) inhibitor, is an effective treatment for chronic plaque psoriasis. Its use is widening to treat other autoinflammatory conditions, but ac...

item.title: Racial and Ethnic Patterns Observed in Inflammatory Bowel Disease Prevalence, Clinical Burden, and Healthcare Resource Utilization in the United States: Findings from an Administrative Claims Database.
item.description: Advances in therapy - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic, immune-mediated gastrointestinal disorder comprised of Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Real-world data on racial differences in dise...

item.title: All-cause and cause-specific mortality in inflammatory bowel disease across the biologic era: a population-based matched cohort study.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is associated with increased mortality, but whether death rates and causes of death have changed across the biologic era is unclear. We aimed to assess all-cause an...

item.title: Association between OLD and IBD: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Meta-analysis - This study aimed to systematically evaluate the bidirectional association between obstructive lung diseases (OLD) [including chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), asthma, and bronchiectasis...

item.title: Hospitalization, ICU admission, and mortality among diabetic patients with inflammatory bowel disease receiving SGLT-2 inhibitors: a retrospective cohort study from the global collaborative network.
item.description: Annals of translational medicine - Patients with coexisting type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) represent a high-risk population with competing comorbidities, complex medication regimens, and overlap...
